name: Process Management with systemd
description: Manage services and processes using systemd, including starting, stopping, enabling, and debugging service units.

steps.title: List running services
steps.description: View all active services and their status.

steps.title: Check a specific service status
steps.description: View detailed status including PID, memory usage, and recent logs.

steps.title: View failed services
steps.description: List all services that have failed to start.
steps.tip: Use 'systemctl reset-failed' to clear the failed state after investigating.

steps.title: Enable a service to start on boot
steps.description: Configure a service to start automatically when the system boots.
steps.tip: The --now flag starts the service immediately in addition to enabling it for boot.

steps.title: View service dependencies
steps.description: See what a service depends on and what depends on it.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Process Management with systemd
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== System State ==="
systemctl is-system-running

echo ""
echo "=== Failed Services ==="
systemctl list-units --type=service --state=failed --no-legend || echo "No failed services"

echo ""
echo "=== Running Services ==="
systemctl list-units --type=service --state=running --no-pager | head -20

echo ""
echo "=== Service Commands ==="
echo "Status:      systemctl status <service>"
echo "Start:       sudo systemctl start <service>"
echo "Stop:        sudo systemctl stop <service>"
echo "Restart:     sudo systemctl restart <service>"
echo "Enable:      sudo systemctl enable --now <service>"
echo "Disable:     sudo systemctl disable --now <service>"
echo "Logs:        journalctl -u <service> -f"
echo "Reload:      sudo systemctl daemon-reload"

faq.question: What is the difference between restart and reload?
faq.answer: Restart stops and starts the service, causing a brief interruption. Reload tells the service to re-read its configuration without stopping, which is supported by some services like Nginx.

faq.question: How do I create a custom systemd service?
faq.answer: Create a unit file in /etc/systemd/system/myapp.service with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections. Then run 'systemctl daemon-reload' and 'systemctl enable --now myapp'.
